reason: Contempt – Godard’s cool, detached look at marriage and art
Alphaville – Godard’s surreal French chip in sci-fi existentialism
Purple Noon – René Clément’s sleek French thriller, existential edge
Belle de jour – Buñuel’s refined, detached exploration of desire
Cinema Paradiso – nostalgic Italian film-lover’s tribute with stylish poise
Roman Holiday – elegant Audrey Hepburn in timeless urban detachment
Manon of the Spring – lyrical French evocation of revenge and fate
Jean de Florette – Provencal study of human folly and social undercurrents
Sense and Sensibility – English period drama with elegant restraint
